summary:
The patient’s episodic chest pain accompanied by palpitations, dyspnea, and fear aligns clinically with panic attack features typical of panic disorder. Based on current guideline-recommended treatments, selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs)—such as citalopram—are preferred as first-line therapy due to superior effectiveness in reducing recurrence and achieving lasting symptomatic improvement compared to beta-blockers alone.

This summary focuses only on essential diagnostic considerations and therapeutic rationale derived directly from available literature without adding external assumptions.
